personas.label: Care recipient
personas.details:
Physically/mentally healthy enough for aging or care at home.
60+ years old, 1 or more chronic conditions
Can benefit from the use of an intervention tool like Vlx
At risk for social isolation
living alone or with a spouse in a home environment 
One or more paid professionals are involved in the individual's care
Some prior experience and comfort level with the use of technology
Limited need for physical assistance

personas.label: Family caregiver
personas.details: Adult child/spouse who has responsibility for aging parent and may still have children at home

personas.label: Care Coordinator
personas.details: A licensed or unlicensed professional who also has good technology, caregiving, and communications skills, and manages multiple care recipients

personas.label: Professional caregiver
personas.details: A licensed professional who gets paid to provide care for one or more care recipients and travels to the individual homes to provide care
An individual aging senior that is responsible for directing their own care
personas.label: Self-Directed Care Recipient

personas.details: Responsible for structuring the care program and delivering value-based outcomes to a group of care recipients
personas.label: Agency or Association

personas.details:
An entity that offers the VLx App as a covered component of their offerings.

Disability Providers
Home Health Agencies
Elder care Law Firms
Strategic Partners
Senior Housing
Care Brokers
Care Coordination Orgs
PACE, LHCSA, CHHA
Government Entities - NYSOFA
Health Care Provider
Health Insurance (Payer)
Wealth Management Firms
Individual
Life Care Planning Law Firm
Employer Group
personas.label: Segment Customer

data.successCriteria:
Clients convert after trial 80%
Deliver trial value outcomes required by the agency
An in the market product that we can justify hiring a salesperson

features.note: This is Wellness guidance driven, which is based on AI algorithms that identify areas of concern for individuals and "nudges" them toward better behavior.

Discovery for finding methods to determine when a user has a fall. Could be the integration of other devices and systems, like Cherish. Could be through acoustics like Sense.ai, camera-based technology, or wearables that provide fall detection capabilities.

Related to ER Avoidance & Acute Event Capture.
